_note: Seed v13 (FEA-2101): brand rename ClosedLoop -> Closedloop in seed-owned display_name/description/install command copy. The version bump is required so upsertCatalogSeed reapplies the renamed copy to existing Desktop installs (rows are skipped while stored seed_version >= incoming). v12 (2026-06-09): adds the built-in Closedloop Web Command Pack as the default installed command protocol boundary. v11's idempotent gstack install commands are preserved.

packs.description: Closedloop's built-in web command protocol pack for PLAN, EXECUTE, DECOMPOSE, EVALUATE_*, GENERATE_PRD, and REQUEST_* commands. Ships with Desktop and brokers commands to the selected local harness; non-run-loop commands can use native Claude or Codex prompt adapters while PLAN/EXECUTE continue through the legacy Claude bridge until that runtime is migrated.

install_commands.claude: echo 'Closedloop Web Command Pack ships with Closedloop Desktop.'
install_commands.codex: echo 'Closedloop Web Command Pack ships with Closedloop Desktop.'
install_commands.cursor: echo 'Closedloop Web Command Pack ships with Closedloop Desktop.'
install_commands.opencode: echo 'Closedloop Web Command Pack ships with Closedloop Desktop.'

uninstall_commands.claude: echo 'Closedloop Web Command Pack is built into Closedloop Desktop.'
uninstall_commands.codex: echo 'Closedloop Web Command Pack is built into Closedloop Desktop.'
uninstall_commands.cursor: echo 'Closedloop Web Command Pack is built into Closedloop Desktop.'
uninstall_commands.opencode: echo 'Closedloop Web Command Pack is built into Closedloop Desktop.'

packs.install_notes: Installed by default from the bundled Desktop resource at resources/command-packs/closedloop-web-command-pack. Cursor and OpenCode are declared as planned adapters until their noninteractive launchers are wired. This pack introduces the Desktop-owned command boundary; it does not yet replace every claude-plugins runtime behavior.

contents.reason: Built-in Desktop command-pack manifest, not a remote skill or plugin tree.

packs.description: Closedloop's own pack: plan, code, code-review, judges, platform, self-learning. The same agents that ship inside the Closedloop platform — multi-agent orchestration with planning, review, and self-learning loops.

uninstall_commands.claude: for p in code code-review judges platform self-learning; do claude plugin uninstall "$p@closedloop-ai" --scope user 2>/dev/null || true; done

packs.install_notes: Installs the five runtime plugins (code, code-review, judges, platform, self-learning) into Claude Code at user scope. The bootstrap plugin is available in the marketplace but excluded from the default install.

packs.description: High-performance CLI proxy that filters and summarizes tool output before it reaches your LLM context. Wraps git/cargo/npm/cargo/docker/kubectl/etc. — saves 60-90% on tokens for common dev commands. Works with any AI coding harness.

install_commands.claude: brew tap rtk-ai/tap && brew install rtk
install_commands.codex: brew tap rtk-ai/tap && brew install rtk

uninstall_commands.claude: brew uninstall rtk && brew untap rtk-ai/tap 2>/dev/null || true
uninstall_commands.codex: brew uninstall rtk && brew untap rtk-ai/tap 2>/dev/null || true

packs.install_notes: Requires Homebrew. After install, prefix dev commands with `rtk` (e.g. `rtk git status`, `rtk cargo test`). For agents to use it automatically, configure the harness's AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md to prefer `rtk`-prefixed commands. The `untap` step is best-effort — fails silently if other taps share the formula.

contents.reason: RTK is a CLI proxy binary, not a skill collection. Installs the `rtk` binary which wraps existing dev tools.

packs.description: Anthropic's official multi-agent code-review plugin: runs specialized review agents with confidence-based scoring to filter false positives. Direct alternative/complement to closedloop's code-review for teams that want a vendor-shipped baseline.
packs.install_notes: Hand-picked from Anthropic's claude-plugins-official marketplace. Installable headlessly via the Claude CLI.

packs.description: Structured workflow for modernizing legacy codebases (COBOL, legacy Java/C++, monolith web apps). Phases: assess → map → extract-rules → reimagine → transform. Ships with agents that own each phase.
Analyzes your codebase and recommends tailored Claude Code automations — hooks, skills, MCP servers, subagents. Excellent first-time-experience companion for new projects.
packs.install_notes: Hand-picked from Anthropic's claude-plugins-official marketplace. After install, invoke its setup skill from a fresh project to get a recommended automation stack.

packs.description: Tools to maintain and improve CLAUDE.md files — audit quality, capture session learnings, keep project memory current. Pairs well with closedloop / gstack which rely on CLAUDE.md for context.

Upstash Context7 MCP server for up-to-date documentation lookup. Pulls version-specific docs + code examples directly from source repositories into your LLM context. Community-managed plugin in Anthropic's official marketplace.
packs.install_notes: MCP server — adds documentation-lookup tools rather than slash commands or agents. Hand-picked from Anthropic's claude-plugins-official marketplace; sourced from external_plugins/context7.

post_install.title: Optional: add an Upstash API key for higher rate limits
post_install.body: Context7 works out of the box on the free tier. If you want higher request limits or private docs, sign up at Upstash and add the API key to your Claude Code config.

packs.description: Methodology + skills framework: brainstorming, TDD, systematic debugging, subagent dev, code review. The single most-installed Claude Code plugin on Anthropic's official marketplace.
packs.install_notes: Installs from Anthropic's official Claude Code plugin marketplace (claude-plugins-official). Codex install is interactive via the Codex /plugins UI — not scripted from CLI.

packs.description: Official Compound Engineering plugin for Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and more. Multi-harness agentic workflows from Every Inc.
packs.install_notes: Installable from Anthropic's official Claude Code plugin marketplace. Codex/Cursor installs follow each harness's plugin format — see README.

packs.description: Garry Tan's opinionated Claude Code setup: 23 tools that serve as CEO/Designer/Eng Manager/Release Manager/Doc Engineer/QA. Includes /autoplan, /ship, /qa, /cso, /review, /codex.

uninstall_commands.claude: rm -rf ~/.claude/skills/gstack && find ~/.claude/skills -maxdepth 2 -name SKILL.md -lname '*gstack*' -print0 2>/dev/null | xargs -0 -I {} dirname {} | xargs -I {} rm -rf {}

packs.install_notes: After the clone lands, gstack's `./setup` is INTERACTIVE — it prompts for several confirmations. The catalog runs the clone + setup, but you may need to answer prompts in the install modal's output pane. Codex install runs `./setup --host codex` after the Claude side. NOTE: gstack's setup creates per-skill symlinks at `~/.claude/skills/<skill-name>/SKILL.md` pointing into the gstack repo (NOT under `~/.claude/skills/gstack/`); the uninstall command removes both the gstack repo AND every dangling symlink it leaves behind.

packs.description: Breakthrough Method for Agile AI-Driven Development: PM/architect/dev/QA agents, PRDs, architecture, stories, acceptance criteria, lifecycle workflow. Installs per-project.
packs.install_notes: Runs in the CURRENT directory and installs per-project. `--yes` alone does NOT skip the directory prompt — `--directory .` is required to bypass interactivity. The installer accepts module/IDE flags too (see README).

packs.description: Curated list of awesome skills, hooks, slash-commands, agent orchestrators, applications, and plugins for Claude Code. A discovery hub, not an installable pack.
packs.placeholder_reason: Catalog repository — browse on GitHub for the actual links. Not installable as a single unit.

packs.description: Route Claude Code through OpenRouter / DeepSeek / Ollama / Gemini and other model providers. Lets you use Claude Code's UX with alternate model backends.
packs.install_notes: Requires Claude Code (`npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code`). After install, configure ~/.claude-code-router/config.json — see README for the schema.

post_install.title: One more step — add your LLM provider keys
post_install.body: Claude Code Router is installed but won't route until you tell it which LLM providers to use. Open the config file and add API keys for OpenRouter, DeepSeek, Ollama, Gemini, or whichever providers you want to route through.

contents.reason: Router CLI tool, not a skill collection. Installs the `ccr` binary.

packs.description: Configuration framework that enhances Claude Code with specialized commands, cognitive personas, and development methodologies. ~30 slash-command commands.
packs.install_notes: Requires pipx (`brew install pipx`). v4.x CLI binary is lowercase `superclaude` (the README still says PascalCase — outdated). The CLI has NO `uninstall` subcommand; cleanup must combine `pipx uninstall` with `rm -rf ~/.claude/commands/sc`. PATH may need ~/.local/bin.

packs.description: Collection of 100+ specialized Claude Code subagents covering frontend, backend, devops, data, security, and more. Cherry-pick — installing all at once gets noisy.
packs.install_notes: Installs the whole collection under ~/.claude/skills/voltagent-subagents/. The upstream README also offers a curl-based installer and per-agent install commands — see the repo to install only specific agents.

packs.description: Anthropic-managed directory of high-quality Claude Code plugins (Superpowers, Compound Engineering, and more). Pre-registered with Claude Code; install individual plugins from it.
packs.install_notes: Pre-registered in fresh Claude Code installs. Use this entry as a pointer to the marketplace's contents (Superpowers, Compound Engineering, etc. — already in this catalog).

packs.description: 313+ skills and plugins across Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI, Cursor, and 8 more coding agents — engineering, marketing, product, compliance, research, business operations.
packs.install_notes: Multi-harness catalog. The clone-and-drop-in pattern works but is heavy — the upstream README also documents an `npx agent-skills-cli add` flow + per-skill cherry-pick. Cherry-pick is recommended over the full clone.
